# merge-descriptors

[![NPM Version][npm-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][downloads-image]][downloads-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Merge objects using descriptors.

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install merge-descriptors
```

## API

```js
var merge = require('merge-descriptors')
```

### merge(destination, source, [redefine])

Merge the property descriptors from `source` onto `destination`. By default,
properties that already exist on the `destination` are not overridden, but this can be changed with the `redefine` parameter.

```js
var dest = {}
var src = Object.create(null, {
  foo: { value: 'bar', enumerable: true }
})

merge(dest, src)

console.log(dest.foo) // => "bar"
```

When `redefine` is `true`, existing properties are overridden by source properties.

```js
var dest = { foo: 'baz' }
var src = Object.create(null, {
  foo: { value: 'bar', enumerable: true }
})

merge(dest, src, true)

console.log(dest.foo) // => "bar"
```

## Example

### Merging objects with getters/setters

```js
var dest = { name: 'object' }
var src = {
  get name() {
    return 'source'
  }
}

Object.defineProperty(dest, 'accessor', {
  get: function() { return 'dst' }
})
Object.defineProperty(src, 'accessor', {
  get: function() { return 'src' }
})

merge(dest, src)

console.log(dest.name)      // => "object"
console.log(dest.accessor)  // => "dst"

merge(dest, src, true)

console.log(dest.name)      // => "source"
console.log(dest.accessor)  // => "src"
```

### Express.js usage example

This module is used within Express.js to merge application settings and other objects:

```js
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

// Express internally uses merge-descriptors to combine properties
// For example, when mounting sub-apps:
var subApp = express()
app.use('/prefix', subApp)
// merge-descriptors helps combine the settings and properties correctly
```
